name: QNI : Potential Spam/Phishing Subject Detected from Multiple Sending Servers
notes:
This rule triggers when multiple sending servers send the same email subject in a period of time which may indicate spam or phishing. 

Note: The custom function ISREPLY returns true or false if a string is the typical subject line of a response email as indicated by having "RE" in the subject.
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Remote, Remote to Local
when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
when the flow matches Content Subject is not N/A
when the flow matches EMAIL::ISREPLY("content subject") = 'false' AQL filter query
when at least 5 flows are seen with the same Content Subject and different SMTP Hello in 15 minutes
thresholds: when at least 5 flows are seen with the same Content Subject and different SMTP Hello in 15 minutes
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Potential Spam/Phishing Subject Detected from Multiple Sending Servers on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow context is Local to Remote, Remote to Local
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
AND when the flow matches Content Subject is not N/A
AND when the flow matches EMAIL::ISREPLY("content subject") = 'false' AQL filter query
AND when at least 5 flows are seen with the same Content Subject and different SMTP Hello in 15 minutes
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Potential Spam/Phishing Subject Detected from Multiple Sending Servers
Event description: This event triggers when multiple sending servers send the same email subject in a period of time which may indicate spam or phishing.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
